title: Garlicky Crisp Thai Wonton Spring-Rolls
description: A crispy and garlicky Thai-inspired appetizer featuring wonton skin, pickled vegetables, and a spicy kick. Serve hot on a stone plate for the ultimate flavor and texture experience.
instructions: Cut the wonton skins into smaller squares, about 3 inches per side.
In a large pan, heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
Add the pickled carrots and cucumbers to the pan and fry until crispy, about 3-4 minutes.
Remove the pickled vegetables from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan, add 1 minced garlic clove and fry until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the minced chicken to the pan and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the soy sauce, fish sauce, and sriracha to the pan and stir to combine.
Return the pickled vegetables to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the wonton skins to the pan and fry until crispy and golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Drain the spring rolls on paper towels and serve hot on a stone plate.
